title: GAO-15-19: SSA Disability Benefits — Physician-Assisted Fraud (2014)
description: Captured gao.gov product page for GAO-15-19 (published Nov 10, 2014; publicly released Dec 10, 2014): What GAO Found, Why GAO Did This Study, and the five Recommendations for Executive Action with their statuses. Found that SSA is vulnerable to physician-assisted fraud because front-line DDS staff struggle to spot suspicious cross-claim patterns, prompt-processing performance measures disincentivize fraud referrals, anti-fraud training is limited/inconsistent, and SSA had not fully evaluated risk from sanctioned physicians' evidence; new analytics initiatives lacked planning, data, and coordination. Of the five recommendations, four are Closed – Implemented and one (review DDS performance standards / promising practices) is Closed – Not Implemented. Highly relevant to 509 DDS fraud-referral workflow and the processing-time-vs-quality tension.

source.title: SSA Disability Benefits: Enhanced Policies and Management Focus Needed to Address Potential Physician-Assisted Fraud (GAO-15-19)

notes: Authoritative: captured gao.gov product page for a published GAO report (GAO-15-19; published Nov 10, 2014, publicly released Dec 10, 2014), containing GAO's own findings and recommendation text. temporal_coverage uses publication year (2014); page lists 5 recommendations (4 Closed – Implemented, 1 Closed – Not Implemented). Capture retains gao.gov site chrome. Base record of the gao-15-19 family (full text in -full); this family has no -summary file. Narrative recommendation table, no declared column schema.
